Michael Caine is interviewed on the set of The Eagle Has Landed in 1971.
Francis Coudrill presents a unique blend of animation and the European Western in this TV classic as Hank Rides Again. 3/13

programmes.show_name: A Day In The Life Of Beryl Cook
programmes.show_description: A look into the world of Beryl Cook, one of Britain's most popular painters-an irreverent grandmother known for bold art featuring royal fantasies and cheeky scenes like a 'female flasher'.
Alex Dyke learns about another popular car from our past when he visits the National Motor Museum at Beaulieu.
The staff of Globelink News are to be the stars of a docusoap, whether they like it or not. As they fend off hand-held cameras, they discover their futures are in doubt.
Globelink is seized by rumours after the announcement of job cuts. In a frantic bid to up the ratings, Gus launches a dumbed-down news.

Jack is in his cottage garden and concludes that no matter how long you have been gardening you can never understand it. He then goes fishing at one of his nearby watermills.
The rocket ship suffers damage from the nitron lamp and is forced to land; Ming recognises a piece of debris and convinces Azura that the party from Earth must be stopped.
The story of Mary Pearcey, who was convicted of murdering her lover's wife and baby in 1890.
Stephen continues his chat with Shoreham's last signalman, while Mike explains signal boxes and interviews Steyning's final porter. S01E02
Jack Hargreaves demonstrates that a horse harness is more than a just pile of straps. He then visits dog trainers, John and Mary Holmes.

When Bill is ill he soon discovers that Faith is unlikely to win a Florence Nightingale award for her bedside manner.
Jane struggles with Laurence leaving. While her meddling mother Bea hovers, friends bombard her with advice from sex clubs and affairs to becoming gay.

Gus is in denial about the imminent demise of Globelink. Helen wrestles with single motherhood and her sexuality, while Sally tries to discover the secret of eternal youth.
Gus is still in denial, George meets Henry's niece, and overcomes his fear of flying. Sally goes in search of an alternative income and Helen discovers she has no secrets.
Explore the sleazy side of 60s London beneath its glittering façade. This documentary takes you deep into Soho to spend time in its seedy bars, strip joints and private clubs.
On a distant planet, a team of scientists is hunted by a monstrous force in Norman J. Warrens cult sci-fi horror. Once labeled a notorious video nasty, it blends terror, gore, and wild fun.
